text:
Once upon a time in a sleepy town hugged by gentle hills, nestled among apple orchards and quaint blacksmith gates, there lived a boy named Tommy who had a secret passion for the stars.

Every night before bed, Tommy would climb the tallest apple tree in his backyard, its branches stretching like arms to the unreachable heavens. There, under the vast, velvety sky, Tommy would build models of galaxies, imagining astronauts discovering planets named in his honor.

One stormy night with rain beating hard against the windows of his family’s cozy, creaky wooden house, Tommy's imagination went wild. The lightning flashed vividly, illuminating his model universe on the wooden plank floor. Below, the raindrops turned the pebbles on the porch into tiny rainbows, transforming the dampness into a melody of wet steps.

In the midst of this fantastical tableau, Tommy thought of his grand ambition — to one day write a science fiction novel that would explore not just space travel but inter
